parsed_review.summary: CP00-751 (Korean Legal Depth P08 Permission Fixture Bridge, risk B, 40 units, RP24.P08.M04.S19-RP24.P08.M06.S14) PASSES read-only review. The descriptor-scoped production_ready claim (production_ready_flag=korean_legal_p08_permission_fixture_bridge_descriptor_verified) is supported by implementation, tests, local/Hermes-gate validation, and enforced no-write/no-real-data boundaries. Verified independently via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) pack identity and composition agree across manifest, review-baseline, registry KOREAN_LEGAL_CP751_PACK_BINDING, KOREAN_LEGAL_CP751_REQUIREMENTS, and the regenerated contract — deliverable distribution {test:2,implementation:17,hermes_evidence:19,claude_review:2}=40 and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:4,Permission And Audit Binding:22,Synthetic Fixture Set:14} match the prompt; (2) row-slice math traced (KOREAN_LEGAL_CP749_EVIDENCE_CONTRACT_ROWS 20 + 2 = 22; slice(18)=M04:4, full=M05:22, slice(0,14)=M06:14) with titles aligned to each unit id; (3) createKoreanLegalCp751P08PermissionFixtureBridgeDescriptor chains from CP750 and defers runtime opening to CP00-752; (4) validators enforce descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no sensitive payloads, no Korean-legal runtime opening, no permission-decision/cross-tenant/audit-body exposure, Hermes/Claude gate + read_only, handoff integrity, and authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false); (5) model.test.js covers CP751 binding, coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract-projection-latest; (6) validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs is non-circular — it imports live source, asserts coverage/descriptor errors empty, and regenerates the contract, whose validation block shows cp751 coverage.valid=true and descriptor.valid=true with errors:[]. No overclaim: manifest runtime_ready=false and production_ready=false are honest; command-evidence shows no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, and all 23 commands exit 0 (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check). Claude remained read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only=true) and non-final; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence list present, auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout, readiness=ready. Pending adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json flags are the expected pre-review placeholders this receipt resolves, not defects. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 informational note that production_ready is descriptor-verification scoped, not runtime/enterprise-trust readiness. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, LDIP results, plan/queue docs) were correctly excluded per changed-file-scope and not attributed to this pack.

findings.title: production_ready is descriptor-verification scoped; runtime/enterprise capability remains deferred
findings.details: The pack's production_ready_flag (korean_legal_p08_permission_fixture_bridge_descriptor_verified) attests descriptor-only verification with all runtime surfaces closed: manifest runtime_ready=false and production_ready=false, and the descriptor authority_boundary.runtime_opening_pack_id defers runtime opening to CP00-752 — continuing a chain where runtime opening is deferred pack-after-pack (CP750->751->752). This is internally consistent, fail-closed, and correctly does NOT overclaim. It is noted only so that goal-closeout/adjudication consumers do not interpret accumulated descriptor-verified packs as a runtime-ready or enterprise-trust product; that requires explicit human final approval outside this receipt (enterprise_trust_claim_closed, human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true).
findings.recommendation: Continue treating this pack's production_ready strictly as descriptor verification. Ensure goal-closeout tracking keeps runtime_ready (0) distinct from descriptor verification so CP completion is not mistaken for an executable product. No code or artifact change required for CP00-751 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; no action required for CP00-751 closeout. Boundary verified consistent across manifest, descriptor authority_boundary, validators, contract validation block, and command evidence.
